about.bio: I build systems that move — production C++ imaging deployed across 130 Jetson Orin edge nodes at Amazon Robotics, multi-robot coverage planners in ROS2, 3D perception pipelines on KITTI, and autonomous drone stacks. Mechanical engineering gave me physical intuition; six years of software gave me the tools to act on it. Now I want to go deeper: full-stack robotics roles where perception, planning, and control meet real hardware.
professionalSummary: Systems Development Engineer at Amazon Robotics — building production C++ imaging services deployed across 130 Jetson Orin edge devices in 6 fulfillment centers, replacing Honeywell scanners at 75% lower cost.

MS in Robotics and Autonomous Systems (4.00 GPA, Graduated With Distinction) from Arizona State University — 3D multi-object tracking, multi-robot coordination, quadrotor control and collision-detection research.

Five years at Infosys: trained 2,000+ engineers as Batch Owner, built ATLAS (ML learning-path tool presented to COO Nandan Nilekani, adopted by Siemens), led the 4-person UI team that won the Philips Healthcare CPQ & PLM contract.

Automobile Engineering undergraduate at Karpagam College of Engineering — technical lead, then team captain of the Formula Student program; also oversaw the Go Kart and vehicle-design competition teams.
Filed U.S. Patent Application No. 63/574,771 with ASU and Dignity Health for a biofeedback system for speech modulation in Parkinson's patients.

responsibilities: Achieved 75% cost reduction ($800→$200/unit) developing Basler camera system in C++ replacing Honeywell scanners
Built C++ imaging service wrapper for Basler Pylon SDK integrated with Jetson Orin edge devices
Deployed solution to 130 edge devices across 6 fulfillment centers using AWS GreenGrass and Docker
Created CloudWatch production alarms and monitoring dashboards for system reliability
Led UWC setup for FastInduct as part of fleet management migration from CDM to UWC platform

projects.title: Amazon Robotics — Basler Imaging Service
projects.description: Built a production C++ imaging service using Basler cameras and the Pylon SDK to replace Honeywell scanners in Amazon fulfillment centers. Deployed across 130 Jetson Orin edge devices via AWS GreenGrass and Docker, cutting hardware costs by 75% ($800 → $200/unit). Integrated CloudWatch monitoring for fleet-wide reliability tracking.
highlights: 75% hardware cost reduction — $800 → $200 per unit
Deployed to 130 edge devices across 6 fulfillment centers
Built C++ Pylon SDK wrapper following Carbon framework patterns
Created CloudWatch production alarms and dashboards

projects.title: Quadrotor Collision Detection Dataset
projects.description: Built a first-of-its-kind dataset for quadrotor collision detection: 1,080+ labeled events across 3 drone types, 2 impact surfaces, and 6 collision speeds. Used a UR5 robotic arm to repeatably stage collisions under slow-motion capture. Integrated Intel RealSense D435 and Velodyne VLP-16 via ROS2 for real-time sensor fusion during free-flight testing.
highlights: 1,080+ unique collision events — 3 drones × 2 surfaces × 6 speeds
25% improvement in ML-based collision detection accuracy
Multi-sensor fusion: RealSense D435 + Velodyne VLP-16 in ROS2
LIOSAM and ORB-SLAM evaluated in Isaac Sim

projects.title: 3D Perception: Tracking & Semantic Segmentation
projects.description: Implemented the AB3DMOT multi-object tracking pipeline with Extended Kalman Filter on the KITTI LiDAR dataset, improving sAMOTA and MOTP metrics by 5%. Separately built a Cylinder3D semantic segmentation model using voxel-based point cloud processing for real-time 3D scene understanding.
highlights: 5% improvement in sAMOTA and MOTP on KITTI benchmark
AB3DMOT tracking pipeline with Extended Kalman Filter
Cylinder3D voxel-based segmentation for real-time inference
Applicable to autonomous navigation perception stacks

projects.title: Multi-Robot Coverage Path Planning
projects.description: Designed and deployed a multi-robot coverage system using the DARP algorithm in Gazebo simulation, achieving 100% warehouse floor coverage with a 40% reduction in exploration time. Implemented ROS2-based path planning with obstacle avoidance in C++ and Python on Raspberry Pi 4. Built a Node.js WebSocket backend for inter-robot communication, cutting coordination latency by 30%.
highlights: 100% coverage with 40% reduction in exploration time (DARP)
30% latency reduction via Node.js WebSocket backend
SILT completed for PX4-based X650 drone fleet
Obstacle-aware path planning in ROS2 on Raspberry Pi 4

projects.title: Mambo Drone — Autonomous Line Following
projects.description: Designed full autonomy stack for the Mambo mini drone in MATLAB/Simulink: fused IMU and downward camera data with a state observer, ran edge detection for line tracking, and implemented state-feedback plus observer-feedback controllers for stable hover and trajectory following.
highlights: Full autonomy stack: perception → state estimation → control
IMU + camera sensor fusion with observer-feedback controller
Edge detection pipeline for visual line tracking
State-feedback controller for stable hover and trajectory following

projects.title: Hexapod Robot — Kinematics & Gait Control
projects.description: Derived DH parameters and formulated complete forward/inverse kinematics for all six legs of a hexapod robot in MATLAB. Implemented gait control enabling independent roll, pitch, yaw, and translation with real-time ground-avoidance and balance constraints.
highlights: Full FK/IK derivation using DH parameters for 6-legged system
6-DOF gait: roll, pitch, yaw, and XYZ translation
Real-time ground-avoidance and static balance constraints
Extensible framework applicable to quadruped platforms

projects.title: ANN vs MPC Controller for Quadrotor
projects.description: Benchmarked a tuned Artificial Neural Network controller against a Model Predictive Controller for quadrotor stabilization. Modeled nonlinear quadrotor dynamics, generated MPC reference trajectories, and trained the ANN to imitate MPC behavior — achieving 85% tracking fidelity at a fraction of the computational cost.
highlights: ANN achieved 85% of MPC tracking performance
Nonlinear quadrotor dynamics modeled from first principles
Significant compute savings vs real-time MPC
Relevant to onboard control on Pixhawk/embedded platforms

projects.title: Parkinson's Biofeedback Device
projects.description: Co-developed a wearable biofeedback device to help Parkinson's patients regulate vocal intensity in real time. Led the full software stack — signal acquisition, adaptive voice filtering (trained on 40 patient samples), and embedded firmware — from concept through clinical prototype. Device improved communication effectiveness in 80% of tested patients and resulted in a filed U.S. patent.
highlights: Patent filed — U.S. Application No: 63/574,771 (April 2024)
80% of tested patients showed measurable improvement
Adaptive voice filter trained on 40 labeled patient samples
35% improvement in patient communication effectiveness

projects.title: ATLAS — ML-Driven Learning Platform
projects.description: Built ATLAS, an ML-driven learning path suggestion platform integrated with Infosys's Wingspan LMS. Developed interactive learning maps with FabricJS, an automated evaluation engine using the MEAN stack, and analytics dashboards in D3.js. The platform personalized learning paths for 2,000+ engineers and increased engagement by 30%.
highlights: 30% increase in user engagement across 2,000+ engineers
Interactive learning maps with FabricJS checkpoints
Automated certification evaluation engine
Analytics dashboards built with D3.js

projects.title: Schematic Design Portal — draw.io + Angular
projects.description: Engineered a custom draw.io build embedded inside an Angular application for a Japanese construction firm's design workflow. Built reusable Angular components for drag-and-drop management of complex hierarchical schematic structures, with optimized parent-child state sync that cut API calls by 45%.
highlights: 45% reduction in API calls through optimized component design
Custom draw.io build embedded in Angular SPA
Reusable drag-and-drop components for hierarchical structures
Seamless parent-child state synchronization

projects.title: Configurator — CPQ Platform for Philips
projects.description: Led a team of four engineers building a React-based CPQ application integrated with Configit Ace APIs and Philips PLM tools. Acted as the client-facing tech lead, managing requirements, sprint planning, and quality gates — achieving 100% test coverage and zero SonarQube issues, while cutting sprint backlog by 40%.
highlights: 40% reduction in sprint backlog through effective coordination
100% unit test coverage with zero SonarQube issues
Led 4-engineer team as client-facing tech lead
Microservices integration with Philips PLM and Configit Ace

projects.title: SUPRA SAE 2018 / Formula Student 2019
projects.description: Captained the college formula student team through two consecutive competitions (SUPRA SAE India 2018 and Formula Student 2019). Drove the vehicle redesign strategy — suspension geometry, powertrain tuning, and weight reduction — that resulted in a 56-position ranking improvement. Managed a cross-functional team of 20+ students.
highlights: 56-position ranking improvement across two competitions
Led 20+ member cross-functional student team
Redesigned suspension geometry and powertrain tuning
Managed full build cycle from design to race day

projects.title: SUPRA SAE 2017
projects.description: Served dual roles as the team's primary driver and technical lead for the 2017 SUPRA SAE competition. Designed powertrain layout, chassis structure, and suspension kinematics from scratch. As the test driver, directly translated handling feedback into suspension and setup changes.
highlights: Dual role: primary driver and technical design lead
Designed powertrain, chassis, and suspension kinematics
Translated driver feedback into suspension setup changes
First competition entry for the team

projects.title: BFKCT Go-Kart 2018
projects.description: Led a small team to design, build, and race a go-kart from scratch in the BFKCT endurance competition. Managed the full project lifecycle — chassis fabrication, engine integration, and race strategy — while also serving as the lead driver during the endurance event.
highlights: Full vehicle built from scratch within competition timeline
Led design, fabrication, and race strategy
Served as lead driver in endurance event
End-to-end project ownership from concept to race

projects.title: PLC Quality Gate — Ashok Leyland
projects.description: Implemented PLC-driven quality gates on the H-Series Engine production line at Ashok Leyland's Hosur plant. Used FMEA to identify critical failure modes, then automated inspection checkpoints that reduced defect escapes by 30% and improved line throughput by 10%. Also optimized the spare parts reorder logic, cutting inventory costs by 20%.
highlights: 30% reduction in production defects via automated quality gates
10% line throughput improvement
20% inventory cost reduction through reorder optimization
FMEA-driven failure mode identification

motorsport.summary: Two years leading motorsport engineering at Karpagam College of Engineering — technical lead year one, team captain year two of the Formula Student program, with concurrent oversight of the Go Kart and vehicle-design competition teams.

roles.team: Formula Student — Team Griffin Riderz, Karpagam College of Engineering
highlights: Captained the team through SUPRA SAE India 2018 and Formula Student 2019 — improved team ranking by 56 positions across two competitions.
Led a 20+ member cross-functional team through the full build cycle: design reviews, fabrication, scrutineering, and race day.
Drove the vehicle redesign strategy — suspension geometry, powertrain tuning, and chassis weight reduction.
Managed full project lifecycle from design freeze through competition.

roles.team: Formula Student — Team Griffin Riderz, Karpagam College of Engineering
highlights: Served as primary driver and technical lead for SUPRA SAE 2017 — the team's first competitive entry.
Designed powertrain layout, chassis structure, and suspension kinematics from scratch in SolidWorks.
Translated direct driver feedback into suspension and setup changes during testing.
Owned the complete technical design process from concept to competition vehicle.
Led design, fabrication, and race strategy for the BFKCT endurance go-kart build.
Served as lead driver during the endurance event.
Full project ownership from concept through race day.

patent.title: Biofeedback System and Method for Speech Modulation
patent.applicant: Arizona Board of Regents on behalf of Arizona State University and Dignity Health
patent.description: Co-developed a wearable biofeedback device helping Parkinson's patients regulate vocal intensity in real time. Led the full software stack — signal acquisition, adaptive voice filtering trained on 40 patient samples, and embedded firmware — from concept through clinical prototype. 80% of tested patients showed measurable improvement in communication effectiveness.
patent.impact: 80% of tested patients showed measurable improvement

stories.title: Automated Angular Assessment Tool
stories.situation: Manual evaluation of 2,000+ Angular trainee submissions was slow and inconsistent — evaluators spent hours copy-pasting projects into master files just to grade them.
stories.task: Streamline the evaluation workflow, improve grading consistency, and enable meaningful feedback at scale.
stories.action: Analyzed the full evaluation workflow end-to-end, designed the architecture, and built an Angular + Node tool that automated extraction, test runs, and report generation. Added support for project variation and evaluator override, then documented and trained the team.
stories.result: 70%+ reduction in evaluation time. 30% capacity boost. Adopted as a best practice across Infosys training programs. Earned an Infosys Insta Award.

stories.title: ATLAS — ML Learning-Path Tool
stories.situation: As Batch Owner I had access to trainee performance data and saw that everyone was following the same learning path regardless of their gaps and strengths.
stories.task: Build a tool that mapped each trainee's progress and recommended personalized next steps.
stories.action: Partnered with two colleagues, negotiated Wingspan API access, built the ML model and Angular UI, integrated it as both a standalone app and an iframe. Presented up through leadership, eventually to then-COO Nandan Nilekani. Onboarded Siemens as an adopting client.
stories.result: +30% user engagement across 2,000+ engineers. Integrated into Infosys Wingspan. Siemens adoption. All three contributors promoted.

stories.title: Philips Healthcare CPQ — Team Lead
stories.principle: Deliver Results · Hire and Develop the Best
stories.situation: Selected to lead a 4-person UI team on a high-visibility Philips Healthcare CPQ proof-of-concept — eight months before leaving for grad school.
stories.task: Pick the team, ramp on Configit, build a compelling POC that wins the long-term contract.
stories.action: Recruited top UI-track graduates via the ETA network. Led the Configit training and built a functional POC during training that we used in the winning pitch. Ran agile cadences, maintained direct comms with POs and architects in the Netherlands and England, and designed a scalable modular UI. Personally reviewed every PR and instituted coding standards that Philips QA later called out as the bar for other streams.
stories.result: Infosys won the long-term Philips Healthcare CPQ and PLM contract. Every sprint commitment met through heavy change-request load.

stories.title: Socket.io Real-Time Tool — Self-Taught Pivot
stories.principle: Learn and Be Curious · Bias for Action
stories.situation: Wanted to expand beyond my immediate job scope into real-time communications. No one asked me to.
stories.task: Self-learn Socket.io and build something real with it.
stories.action: Read the docs, built small apps on my own time, and created a real-time code-review tool. When COVID shifted all training online, pivoted the tool into a real-time attendance and quiz application.
stories.result: The attendance tool became core to Infosys online training programs. Canvas.js learning maps built from the same self-study powered ATLAS's most-loved feature.

stories.title: Standardizing Assessment Question Papers
stories.situation: Angular question papers varied wildly across batches — some over-indexed on CSS trivia, others on obscure edge cases. Grading was incomparable across cohorts.
stories.task: Make the case for a minimum testing specification and get team alignment.
stories.action: Analyzed existing papers and mapped coverage gaps with data. Opened a team discussion, acknowledged creativity concerns, and argued that fairness required a baseline — that CSS-heavy papers were measuring the wrong things for an Angular assessment. Drafted a proposal and iterated.
stories.result: Team aligned on a standard spec. Trainees reported higher confidence. Content creators worked faster. Evaluations became comparable across cohorts.
